title: Alternative path for storage.conf , containers.conf
body:
buildah expects storage.conf, container.conf from /use/share/containers, /etc/containers, $HOME/.config/containers and XDG_CONFIG_HOME. Can we add an option to specify alternate path to user configs from command line or through environment variables . This will be useful for automated  builds as unprivileged user.

CONTAINERS_CONFIG_DIR=/mydir
Or
buildah/podman  --containers-conf=/mydir

comments.body:
CONTAINERS_CONF=/mydir works now, but only for containers.conf, not storage.conf, registries.conf ...

I would guess XDG_CONFIG_DIR, would be the way to handle this, but I don't believe we do this now.

HOME=/mydir podman ...
Would also cause us to look for the files in /mydir/.config/containers/*

Great!! Will HOME=/mydir pickup storage.conf as well?   While XDG_CONFIG_DIR works, it implies that it is user's home directory which isn't true in my case, I guess HOME also implies the same. IMO there should a clear flag stating the path of container configs without implying that it is user's home. I guess with move to podman SDK, i could specify config paths. My yocot toolchain with container-tools , configs, and writable paths are completely sandboxed in code repo. I should start contributing 🙂 .
